name: add-gmail-tool
tagline: Wires a Gmail MCP server into agent containers via OneCLI-managed OAuth credential stubs, covering Dockerfile, DB-backed group config, and.
verdict: Strong operational skill: concrete commands, named files, explicit failure-path troubleshooting, and mostly-idempotent steps. No secrets ever leave the machine — OneCLI injects real tokens out of band. Weak points: no negative-trigger scope guidance, no skill versioning/changelog, jq used unchecked, and bundled reference files (REMOVE.md, test files) can't.

highlights.summary: Description states what it does at length but gives no explicit trigger phrasing/situation an agent would match a user request against.
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill language anywhere in description or body.
add-mount is explicitly stated idempotent, but the Phase 3 'ncl groups config add-mcp-server' registration step has no stated duplicate/idempotency check.
